noi_chen_keqing_2009.gold:
Chen Keqing (陈可卿), born in 1991 in Shaoxing, Zhejiang, is a technology entrepreneur and computer scientist. While attending Shaoxing No. 1 Middle School, he won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking third in China, and was subsequently admitted to Tsinghua University.

In 2012, Chen joined Megvii (旷视科技) as its fifth employee. During his eight-year tenure, he led the development of the Face++ cloud platform and pioneered mobile face-unlocking solutions for major smartphone brands, including the first mass-produced 3D structured light technology for Android. In 2020, he founded JIIOV Technology (极豪科技), where he serves as CEO. The company specializes in AI-driven sensing chips and integrated fingerprint recognition solutions for the global smartphone market.

noi_wu_jiajun_2009.gold:
Wu Jiajun (吴佳俊) is a computer scientist and an Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Stanford University. In 2009, as a student at East China Normal University Second High School (上海市华师大二附中), he won a gold medal at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking sixth in China. He was subsequently admitted to the "Yao Class" at Tsinghua University, where he earned his bachelor's degree and received the Tsinghua University Top Grade Scholarship. 

He completed his PhD in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT). His research focuses on computer vision, machine learning, and computational cognitive science. His honors include the IJCAI Computers and Thought Award, the ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award Honorable Mention, and the Samsung AI Researcher of the Year.

noi_li_xinye_2009.gold:
Li Xinye (李新野) is a competitive programmer and quantitative finance professional. While attending Shantou Jinshan Middle School in Guangdong Province, he won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking second nationally. Following this achievement, he was admitted to Tsinghua University in 2010, where he studied in the Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (commonly known as the "Yao Class").

After graduating from Tsinghua, Li entered the financial sector, working as a quantitative researcher at Citadel Securities. He later transitioned into the private equity industry, focusing on quantitative trading and investment. He has also been a prominent contributor on Chinese social media platforms such as Zhihu, where he shares insights on computer science, education, and the quantitative finance industry.

noi_wu_yi_2009.gold:
Wu Yi is a computer scientist and former competitive programmer. He earned a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while attending Changzhou Senior High School of Jiangsu Province. In 2010, he served as captain of the Chinese national team and won a silver medal at the International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI). He was subsequently admitted to the "Yao Class" at Tsinghua University and later completed a PhD in computer science at the University of California, Berkeley, under the supervision of Stuart Russell.

Wu was an early researcher at OpenAI, where he worked on reinforcement learning. In 2020, he returned to China as an assistant professor at Tsinghua University’s Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences. He founded the startup Border Technology, which was acquired by Ant Group in 2024. In 2026, he joined Meta’s Superintelligence Lab.

noi_lai_luhang_2009.gold:
Lai Luhang (赖陆航) is a competitive programmer and 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) gold medalist from Hangzhou No. 2 High School. He ranked fourth overall in the 2009 NOI and subsequently represented China at the 22nd International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI 2010) in Canada, where he won a gold medal and ranked 11th globally.

Following his success in national competitions, Lai was admitted to the School of Electronics Engineering and Computer Science at Peking University without taking the Gaokao. During his collegiate career, he competed in the 2014 ACM-ICPC World Finals, earning a silver medal and ranking 6th. Known in the competitive programming community by the handles "xfastx" and "lordxfastx," he earned the nickname "the god who never makes mistakes in the computer room" for his high accuracy.

noi_pan_yuchao_2009.gold:
Pan Yuchao (潘宇超) is a competitive programmer and researcher who graduated from Shaoxing No. 1 Middle School in Zhejiang Province. In 2009, he won a gold medal at the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking seventh nationally. He subsequently represented China at the 2010 International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI) in Waterloo, Canada, where he earned a silver medal.

He was admitted to the Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (Yao Class) at Tsinghua University. As a member of the Tsinghua team "Y.E.S," he won a gold medal (third place) at the 2011 ACM-ICPC World Finals. He continued his studies at Tsinghua as a graduate student in the Machine Learning and Computational Biology Group, where his research focused on computational protein design and deep learning applications in medical imaging.

noi_wang_yining_2009.gold:
Wang Yining (汪一宁) is a computer scientist and academic who won a gold medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking 10th while representing the Nanjing Normal University Affiliated High School. He also earned a gold medal at the 2008 Asia-Pacific Olympiad in Informatics (APIO).

He attended Tsinghua University from 2010 to 2014 as a student in the "Yao Class" (Computer Science), where he received the Yao Award, the program's highest honor. He later earned a PhD in Machine Learning from Carnegie Mellon University in 2019. Professionally, he served as an assistant professor at the University of Florida before joining the University of Texas at Dallas, where he is an associate professor of operations management. His research focuses on machine learning, reinforcement learning, and sequential decision-making under uncertainty.

noi_li_kaiwei_2009.gold:
Li Kaiwei (李恺威) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer. As a student at Hangzhou Xuejun High School, he studied under coach Xu Xianyou and won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking 13th nationally. This achievement earned him a recommendation for admission to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University, where he enrolled in 2010.

At Tsinghua, Li was a member of the university’s supercomputing team, which won the overall championship at the 2015 World Student Supercomputer Challenge (ASC15). After completing his undergraduate studies, he continued at Tsinghua University to pursue a master's degree within the Institute of High Performance Computing.

noi_mo_tao_2009.gold:
Mo Tao (莫涛) is a prominent Chinese competitive programmer best known for developing **Mo's Algorithm** (莫队算法), a widely used offline technique for processing interval queries. He attended **Changsha Changjun High School** in Hunan Province, where he achieved national recognition by winning the gold medal and ranking first overall at the **2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI)**.

He subsequently enrolled at **Tsinghua University**, where he continued his success in competitive programming. In 2013, he competed in the **ACM-ICPC World Finals** in Saint Petersburg as a member of the Tsinghua team "Again And Again," earning a bronze medal (ranked 12th globally). Within the informatics community, he is often referred to by the nickname "Mo Dui" (莫队), a title derived from the algorithm he popularized.

noi_li_yuliang_2009.gold:
Li Yuliang (李宇亮) is a computer scientist specializing in networked systems and cloud infrastructure. He attended Hangzhou Xuejun High School, where he won a gold medal in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking 11th nationally. He was subsequently admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University, graduating in 2014. During his undergraduate studies, he received a Student Research Competition award at ACM SIGCOMM 2014.

Li earned his Ph.D. in Computer Science from Harvard University in 2020, following research periods at the University of Southern California and Yale University under advisor Minlan Yu. His doctoral dissertation on hardware-software codesign for cloud networks received the 2020 ACM SIGCOMM Doctoral Dissertation Award. He currently works on the Network Infrastructure team at Google Cloud, focusing on high-performance transport and clock synchronization.

noi_li_xiaoxiao_2009.gold:
Li Xiaoxiao (李晓潇) is a Chinese computer scientist and quantitative investor. While a student at Fuzhou No. 1 High School, he won a gold medal (ranking 8th nationally) at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and was selected for the national training team. He was subsequently admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University in 2010. As an undergraduate, he co-authored research published in top-tier venues such as SIGCOMM.

Li earned his PhD in Artificial Intelligence and Deep Learning from the Chinese University of Hong Kong, where he was advised by SenseTime founder Tang Xiaoou and conducted research at the MMLab. His academic work in deep learning has garnered over 8,500 citations. In 2019, he co-founded the initial team of what became Beijing Zhengding Private Equity (正定私募) alongside fellow Tsinghua alumni and NOI medalists Mo Tao and Jiang Linhao. He currently serves as the firm's co-founder and algorithm lead.

noi_chen_gaoyuan_2009.gold:
Chen Gaoyuan (陈高远) is a competitive programmer who won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking ninth nationally. He attended Beijing 101 Middle School, where he also earned first prizes in the 2008 and 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and won the 2009 Canadian Computing Competition (Beijing region).

He was admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University in 2011. As a member of the university's programming teams, he won the runner-up title at the 2012 Cross-Strait Tsinghua and HKUST Programming Contest and competed in overseas ACM-ICPC regionals. A high-ranking competitive programmer, he achieved the "Target" rank on Topcoder. He later pursued a Master’s degree at the University of Southern California.

noi_zhang_fang_2009.gold:
Zhang Fang (张放) is a computer scientist specializing in quantum error correction and fault tolerance. He gained prominence as a competitive programmer at the High School Affiliated to Renmin University of China, winning gold medals at the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in 2009, 2010, and 2011. In 2011, he also achieved first place at the China Team Selection Contest (CTSC).

He was admitted to Tsinghua University’s Computer Science Experimental Class (Yao Class) in 2012, graduating in 2016. He subsequently earned a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science from the University of Michigan in 2020. Zhang served as a quantum scientist at Alibaba Group in the United States from 2020 to 2023. As of 2024, he is an associate researcher at Zhongguancun Laboratory and is affiliated with the Quantum Software Research Center at Tsinghua University.

noi_lyu_weicong_2009.gold:
Lyu Weicong (吕伟聪) is a computer scientist and academic who won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) as a student at Nanjing Foreign Language School. His performance earned him a recommendation for admission to Peking University, where he enrolled in Yuanpei College and completed a double degree in Statistics and Economics in 2015.

He subsequently pursued graduate studies at the University of Wisconsin–Madison, earning an M.S. in Computer Science (2020), an M.A. in Mathematics (2023), and a Ph.D. in Educational Psychology with a focus on quantitative methods (2023). As of 2024, he serves as an Assistant Professor of Educational Testing, Assessment, and Evaluation at the University of Macau. His research focuses on psychometrics, item response theory, and Bayesian methods.

noi_lin_yankai_2009.gold:
Lin Yankai (林衍凯) is a computer scientist and an associate professor at the Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China. He first gained prominence as a gold medalist in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while representing Shantou Jinshan Middle School. He subsequently attended Tsinghua University, where he earned his bachelor’s degree in 2014 and his Ph.D. in 2019 under the supervision of Professor Maosong Sun.

Following his doctoral studies, Lin served as a senior researcher at Tencent WeChat AI from 2019 to 2022. His research focuses on natural language processing, large language models, and knowledge representation, with numerous publications in top-tier conferences such as ACL, EMNLP, and NeurIPS. Lin also serves as a member of the CCF NOI Scientific Committee.

noi_guo_jiabao_2009.gold:
Guo Jiabao (郭家宝), also known by his online handle BYVoid, is a software engineer and author. While a student at Henan Experimental High School, he won a gold medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and was subsequently admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University in 2010.

Guo is the author of the *Node.js Development Guide* (2012) and created OpenCC (Open Chinese Convert), an open-source tool for conversion between Simplified and Traditional Chinese. He also contributed significantly to the Linux input method `ibus-pinyin`. Professionally, he has worked as a software engineer for Google in its Zurich, New York, and Tokyo offices. He is also recognized for his extensive international travel, having visited over 100 countries.

noi_chen_jianfei_2009.gold:
Chen Jianfei (陈键飞) is a computer scientist and an associate professor in the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University. He first gained prominence as a gold medalist in the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while representing the Affiliated High School of Shandong Normal University.

Chen completed his undergraduate studies in computer science at Tsinghua University in 2014 and earned his PhD from the same institution in 2019. His research focuses on efficient machine learning, specifically quantized neural networks, stochastic optimization, and deep generative models. He has developed several scalable training systems and contributed to open-source projects such as ActNN, WarpLDA, and ZhuSuan. Chen received the CCF Excellent Doctoral Dissertation Award in 2019 and serves as an associate editor for IEEE TPAMI.

noi_song_wenjie_2009.gold: Song Wenjie (宋文杰) is a competitive programmer from Jiangsu Province Suzhou High School (江苏省苏州中学). In 2009, while a second-year student in high school, he won a gold medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), ranking 14th nationally with a total score of 373. Following this achievement, he was selected as a member of the IOI 2010 China National Training Team. His performance was noted as a significant milestone for his school's informatics program, serving as a benchmark for subsequent students in the following decade. He was among the top contestants from Jiangsu Province who received admission offers from prestigious Chinese universities, including Tsinghua and Peking University, through the competition's recommendation system.

noi_s_wu_shang_2009.gold:
Wu Shang (吴尚) is a competitive programmer who attended Nanjing Foreign Language School. In 2009, while a tenth-grade student, he earned a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), finishing first in the silver medal category. Following this achievement, he was recruited by Peking University with the flexibility to choose his major.

In 2010, Wu continued his competitive success by winning gold medals at the Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO), the China Team Selection Contest (CTSC), and the 27th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2010), where he ranked 10th nationally. He began learning computer programming in the fifth grade and achieved a first-prize ranking in the Jiangsu provincial primary school division by the sixth grade.

noi_s_fan_haoqiang_2009.gold:
Fan Haoqiang (范浩强) is a Chinese computer scientist and entrepreneur. While a student at the High School Affiliated to Renmin University of China, he earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and later won a gold medal (ranking second globally) at the 2011 International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI). 

In 2011, he joined Megvii (Face++) as its sixth employee while still in high school. He later graduated from Tsinghua University’s "Yao Class" (Computer Science Experimental Class), where he maintained top academic standing while working at Megvii. At Megvii, he served as Research General Manager and led teams to multiple international championships in computer vision. In late 2024, he co-founded Force Bot (原力灵机), an embodied intelligence startup. He also created viral large language model-based games.

noi_s_liu_yining_2009.gold: Liu Yining (刘艺宁) is a competitive programmer who represented Changsha Yali Middle School (湖南省长沙雅礼中学) in national informatics competitions. In 2009, he won a silver medal (second prize) at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), ranking 22nd overall. He also earned first-prize honors in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in both 2008 and 2009. Due to his achievements in these competitions, he was admitted to Tsinghua University in 2010 as a recommended student (保送生).

noi_feng_qiwei_2009.gold:
Feng Qiwei (冯齐纬) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer. While a student at Zhongshan No.1 Middle School in Guangdong, he achieved significant success in informatics competitions, winning gold medals at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in both 2009 and 2010. In 2010, he represented China at the 22nd International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI) in Canada, where he earned a gold medal and ranked 10th globally.

Following his competition success, Feng was admitted to Tsinghua University, where he studied at the Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (Yao Class). During his undergraduate studies, he co-authored research on deep learning and medical image analysis, including a 2014 paper presented at the IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech and Signal Processing (ICASSP).

noi_s_ni_jinan_2009.gold:
Ni Jinan (倪际楠) is a competitive programmer from Fujian Province, China. He attended the High School Affiliated to Fujian Normal University (福建师大附中), where he was a member of the school's informatics competition team. In 2008, as a tenth-grade student, he earned a first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and was selected for the Fujian provincial team. 

In 2009, Ni won a silver medal (second prize) at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He also secured a first prize in the NOIP 2009 senior group. Based on his competitive achievements, he was recommended for admission (baosong) to Peking University in 2011, where he enrolled in the Electronic Information Science program.

noi_s_zhou_yisong_2009.gold:
Zhou Yisong (周逸松) is a competitive programmer who won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while representing Jilin No. 1 Middle School (吉林省吉林一中). Following his success in the national competition, he enrolled in the School of Electronics Engineering and Computer Science at Peking University in 2010.

As an undergraduate, he continued competing in programming contests. He was a member of the Peking University "NeverAC" team, which earned gold medals at the 2011 ACM-ICPC Asia Regional contests in both the Dalian and Shanghai sites. His performance contributed to Peking University's standing in the international collegiate programming circuit during that period.

noi_s_fan_shunhao_2009.gold:
Fan Shunhao (范顺豪) is a Chinese entrepreneur and computer scientist. As a student at Hangzhou Xuejun High School, he earned silver medals at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in 2009 and 2010, and a gold medal at the Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO). He was subsequently admitted to the Computer Science Experimental Class (Yao Class) at Tsinghua University, where he served as the inaugural president of the Yao Class Student Union.

In 2016, Fan founded Beijing Hongyu Technology, a startup specializing in 3D operating systems for augmented and virtual reality, including the IrisView system. He also serves as a director for Hangzhou Heshun Technology Co., Ltd., his family's industrial film business. Fan has been recognized on the Forbes China 30 Under 30 list.

noi_he_pufan_2009.gold:
He Pufan (何朴藩) is a Chinese competitive programmer and informatics educator who attended Yali High School (湖南省长沙雅礼中学) in Changsha, Hunan. He achieved national prominence as a two-time gold medalist in the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), winning gold in 2009 as a tenth-grade student and again in 2010.

A member of the 2009 and 2011 National Training Teams (国家集训队), He contributed to the field as a problem setter for national-level competitions. His authored works include several well-known informatics problems used in training and online judges, such as "Book Pile" (书堆) from the 2009 training set, and "Feifei Xia" (飞飞侠) and "Jumping Chess" (跳跳棋) from the 2011 training set.

noi_s_fu_qiang_2009.gold: Fu Qiang (傅强) is a competitive programmer who represented Shandong Experimental High School (山东省实验中学) at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009). During the competition, he was a second-year high school student (Grade 11). He earned a silver medal with a total score of 336, ranking among the top performers in the national finals. His contestant identification for the event was CCF-NOI09-028. No further verified information regarding his subsequent academic career or professional life is available.

noi_s_wei_ming_2009.gold:
Wei Ming (魏铭) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer. While attending Shandong Normal University Affiliated High School, he won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and subsequently earned a gold medal (ranking third nationally) at the 2010 NOI. 

He was admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University in 2011 via recommendation. During his undergraduate studies, he competed in the ACM-ICPC, securing a regional third-place finish. Professionally, he joined the artificial intelligence company Megvii (旷视科技), where he specialized in performance optimization for heterogeneous platforms. He is originally from Zibo, Shandong Province.

noi_s_yang_huan_2009.gold: Yang Huan (杨欢) is a competitive programmer from Tianjin, China. While a student at Tianjin Nankai High School, he achieved significant success in national informatics competitions. In 2009, he won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) with a score of 335. His competitive record also includes a bronze medal at the 2008 NOI and a first-prize award in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). Beyond informatics, he was recognized in the 2009 High School Mathematics League as an award winner in the Tianjin division.

noi_s_wang_zhengyu_2009.gold:
Wang Zhengyu (王正宇) is a computer scientist and quantitative researcher. He attended Northeast Yucai School in Liaoning Province, where he earned a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In 2010, he was admitted to Tsinghua University as a recommended student, joining the Computer Science Experimental Class (Yao Class).

After graduating from Tsinghua in 2014, Wang pursued doctoral studies at Harvard University. He received his Ph.D. in Computer Science in 2019 under the supervision of Jelani Nelson, with a dissertation focused on streaming algorithms and graph connectivity. His research contributions include work on randomized algorithms, dimensionality reduction, and data structures for massive-scale data processing. Following his academic career, he became a quantitative researcher at Citadel Securities.

noi_s_deng_yongxing_2009.gold:
Deng Yongxing (邓永行) is a technology professional and former competitive programmer. He earned a silver medal in the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) as a student at East China Normal University Second High School (华师大二附中) in Shanghai. In 2011, he was admitted to Stanford University, where he received both a Bachelor of Science and a Master of Science in Computer Science in 2015, earning the Terman Award for academic excellence. He later obtained an MBA with High Distinction from Harvard Business School in 2021.

Deng’s career includes roles as an engineering manager at Slack and software engineering internships at Dropbox and Asana. In 2020, he co-founded the real estate appraisal startup Aloft, serving as its Chief Technology Officer until 2023. He currently serves as a Member of Technical Staff and tech lead at Anthropic.

noi_s_liu_yukun_2009.gold: Liu Yukun (刘宇坤) is a Chinese competitive programmer who represented Baoding No. 2 Middle School (河北省保定二中) in informatics competitions. In July 2009, while a second-year student in senior high school (Grade 11), he won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009). Later that year, as a third-year student (Grade 12), he earned a first prize in the Senior Group of the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP 2009) in Hebei. His performance in these national-level competitions established him as a prominent student competitor in the field of computer science within his province during his high school years.

noi_s_pan_zheyi_2009.gold:
Pan Zheyi (潘哲逸) is a computer science researcher and a 2009 silver medalist of the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He attended Fujian Normal University Affiliated High School, where he was a member of the "Lingyun" class and was selected for the Fujian provincial informatics team during his first year of high school.

He pursued his higher education at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, enrolling in the prestigious ACM Class of the Zhiyuan College. He received his Ph.D. in 2020 under the supervision of Professor Yu Yong, focusing on spatio-temporal data mining and urban computing. Pan is currently an associate researcher at the JD Intelligent Cities Research Institute. His accolades include the 2014 Microsoft-IEEE Young Scholar Award, being named a 2021 Global AI Chinese Young Star, and selection for the 2024 Beijing Science and Technology Star Program.

noi_s_shu_ruiwen_2009.gold:
Shu Ruiwen (舒睿文) is a mathematician and assistant professor at the University of Georgia. He attended the High School Affiliated to Renmin University of China, where he earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the National High School Mathematics League. 

He completed his undergraduate studies at Peking University, where he received the Outstanding Freshman Scholarship and a bronze medal in the 2013 S.-T. Yau College Student Mathematics Contests. Shu earned his Ph.D. in mathematics from the University of Wisconsin–Madison in 2018. He subsequently held postdoctoral research positions at the University of Maryland, College Park, and the University of Oxford. His research focuses on collective dynamics, kinetic equations, and numerical methods for hyperbolic problems and uncertainty quantification.

noi_s_yan_songbai_2009.gold:
Yan Songbai (严松柏) is a computer scientist and software engineer. While attending Chongqing No. 1 Middle School, he won a silver medal (Second Prize) at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He was subsequently admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Peking University, where he earned his bachelor's degree in 2014.

Yan continued his education at the University of California, San Diego (UCSD), where he completed a PhD in Computer Science. His doctoral research focused on machine learning theory and algorithms, particularly active learning and human-in-the-loop systems. He has published numerous papers at major conferences such as NeurIPS, ICML, and NIPS. Following his graduate studies, he joined Google as a software engineer based in Waterloo, Canada.

noi_s_lyu_xiao_2009.gold:
Lyu Xiao (吕潇) is a computer scientist and competitive programmer. As a student at the High School Attached to Shandong Normal University (山东师大附中), he was a prominent contestant in the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), winning silver medals in both 2008 and 2009. His performance in the 2009 competition, where he ranked 33rd nationally, earned him a recommendation for admission to Peking University. 

He enrolled in the School of Electronics Engineering and Computer Science (EECS) at Peking University, where he completed his undergraduate studies and subsequently obtained a master's degree in 2017. Following his graduation, he joined Microsoft Research Asia (MSRA), where he has shared his professional expertise in fields such as artificial intelligence and deep learning.

noi_s_sun_xiangyan_2009.gold:
Sun Xiangyan (孙祥彦) is a computer scientist and competitive programmer. While attending Hefei No. 1 High School in Anhui Province, he was a member of the provincial informatics team and won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He subsequently studied computer science at Fudan University, where he earned both his bachelor's and master's degrees.

In 2014, Sun won the championship of the 3rd Microsoft "Beauty of Programming" (编程之美) challenge, a prestigious competition co-organized by Microsoft and IEEE. His winning project utilized algorithms to predict urban air quality based on historical meteorological and traffic data. Professionally, he has worked as a researcher and engineer at Accutar Biotech (冰洲石生物科技), an AI-driven drug discovery company, where he has contributed to patented technologies in computational chemistry and retrosynthesis.

noi_s_zhou_yichao_2009.gold:
Zhou Yichao (周奕超) is a computer scientist and competitive programmer from Shanghai. While attending the High School Affiliated to Fudan University, he earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a gold medal at the 2010 NOI. In 2011, he represented the Chinese national team at the International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI), where he won a silver medal.

He graduated from the "Yao Class" at Tsinghua University in 2015 with a degree in Computer Science and Technology. He subsequently earned a Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley, in 2020, specializing in 3D computer vision and geometric deep learning. Professionally, he is a research scientist at Apple, where he has worked on the SLAM team for ARKit and currently focuses on video world models and 3D perception.

noi_s_wu_hao_2009.gold: Wu Hao (吴豪) is a competitive programmer who earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in China. At the time of the competition, he was a second-year student (Grade 11) at Wuhan Iron and Steel No. 3 High School (湖北省武钢三中) in Hubei province. Representing the Hubei provincial team, he achieved a total score of 309 points to secure his silver medal ranking. He also received a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for the Hubei region.

noi_s_huang_cai_2009.gold:
Huang Cai (黄偲) is a computer scientist and entrepreneur who earned a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) as a student at Baoding No. 2 Middle School in Hebei Province. He was subsequently recommended for admission to the ACM Class at Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU).

During his undergraduate studies, Huang won the championship at the 2012 Microsoft "Beauty of Programming" National Challenge and served as the inaugural vice chairman of the CCF SJTU Student Chapter. In his professional career, he served as a technical architect and Vice President of Product at the educational technology company SmartStudy (智课教育). He later co-founded Fangti Education (方体语文), an online platform specializing in Chinese language and literature education for K-12 students.

noi_s_hu_zhangguangda_2009.gold:
Hu Zhangguangda is a computer scientist and competitive programmer. While a student at Fuzhou No.1 High School, he earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). He subsequently attended Shanghai Jiao Tong University as a member of the elite ACM Class. In 2012, he competed in the 36th ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest (ICPC) World Finals, where his team won a gold medal and the Asian Championship.

Hu Zhangguangda later joined Alibaba Group as an algorithm researcher. His professional work focuses on machine learning, recommender systems, and autonomous agents. He has published extensively in top-tier academic venues, including NeurIPS, KDD, and AAAI, contributing to the development of large-scale industrial recommendation and search algorithms.

noi_s_jiang_linhao_2009.gold:
Jiang Linhao (蒋林浩) is a quantitative finance professional and the founder of Zhengding Investment (北京正定私募). He first gained prominence as a competitive programmer at the No. 2 High School of East China Normal University, where he won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a gold medal at the Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO). He served as the class monitor of the school’s renowned 2010 "top class," from which all 54 students were recommended to elite universities.

Jiang was recommended to Tsinghua University, where he earned dual bachelor's degrees in Computer Science and Economics, followed by a master's degree in Artificial Intelligence. At Tsinghua, he led the university's supercomputing team to a second-place finish in the ASC Student Supercomputer Challenge. After working as a quantitative researcher at firms including Shenzhen First Jingguang Asset Management and Shanghai Gangxing Investment, he co-founded Zhengding Investment in 2022 alongside fellow informatics medalists Mo Tao and Li Xiaoxiao. By 2024, the firm had grown into a prominent quantitative private equity fund with over 10 billion RMB in assets under management.

noi_s_xiang_shang_2009.gold: Xiang Shang (向上) is a competitive programmer from Changsha Yali Middle School (湖南省长沙雅礼中学) in Hunan Province. He achieved significant success in national informatics competitions during his high school years. In 2009, he won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009). The following year, he earned a gold medal at the 2010 China Team Selection Contest (CTSC 2010) and a silver medal at the 27th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2010). Based on his competition record, he was recommended for admission to Tsinghua University in 2011.

noi_s_ma_hao_2009.gold:
Ma Hao (马浩) is a competitive programmer from Henan Experimental High School (河南省实验中学). In 2009, while in the eleventh grade, he won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) with a score of 290. That same year, he earned a provincial first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP 2009). 

In 2010, Ma represented his school at the Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO), where he also secured a silver medal. Due to his achievements in informatics competitions, he was recommended for admission (保送) to Fudan University in 2010. During his high school career, he was a member of the school's advanced informatics competition group coached by Chang Qingwei.

noi_s_qian_qiao_2009.gold:
Qian Qiao (钱桥) is a computer science researcher and former competitive programmer. He attended Tianjin Nankai High School, where he won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a gold medal (ranking 5th nationally) at the 2010 NOI. He also earned a second prize in the 2009 National High School Mathematics League.

In 2011, Qian was admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University. As a member of the Conversational AI Group (CoAI), his research focused on sentiment analysis and dialogue systems. He co-authored several academic papers, including "Assigning Personality/Profile to a Chatting Machine for Coherent Conversation Generation" (IJCAI 2018), and collaborated with Sogou on generative conversational models. He graduated from Tsinghua University in 2018.

noi_s_bai_yanbo_2009.gold:
Bai Yanbo (白彦博) is a competitive programmer and computer scientist. While a student at Xi'an No. 83 Middle School (陕西省西安83中), he earned a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). Based on his competition achievements, he was admitted to Fudan University in 2010 through the recommendation (baosong) program for Olympiad winners.

He studied at Fudan's School of Computer Science and graduated in 2014. During his undergraduate years, he gained media attention for his relationship with classmate Wei Chuchu, a philosophy student; the couple married prior to graduation and were featured in the university's 2014 graduation ceremony red carpet event.

noi_s_ren_chunxu_2009.gold:
Ren Chunxu (任春旭) is a computer scientist and game AI engineer. While attending Sichuan Mianyang Middle School, he won a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He subsequently enrolled at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, where he became a member of the university's ACM-ICPC team. In 2011, he competed as part of the team "TrustMe," winning a gold medal at the ACM-ICPC Beijing Regional Contest.

Ren later joined NetEase, specifically working within the NetEase Fuxi Lab as a Game AI Engineer. His professional work focuses on deep reinforcement learning and multi-agent systems. He has co-authored research papers on applying these technologies to complex game environments, such as basketball simulations, and holds several patents related to the training and control of intelligent agents in video games.

noi_s_xiang_ziqing_2009.gold:
Xiang Ziqing (向子卿) is a mathematician and associate professor at Southern University of Science and Technology (SUSTech). While attending Harbin Normal University Affiliated High School, he won a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the 2009 National High School Mathematics League.

He earned a B.Sc. in Computer Science from Shanghai Jiao Tong University in 2014, where he was a Junzheng Scholar. He subsequently obtained a Ph.D. in Mathematics from the University of Georgia in 2019. After serving as a postdoctoral research scholar at Academia Sinica, he joined the Department of Mathematics at SUSTech in 2022. His research focuses on algebraic combinatorics, design theory, and representation theory. In 2022, he was awarded the Kirkman Medal by the Institute of Combinatorics and its Applications.

noi_s_wang_mo_2009.gold: Wang Mo (王谟) is a competitive programmer and mathematician who attended Wuhan Iron and Steel No. 3 High School (武钢三中) in Hubei Province. In 2009, while in his second year of high school, he won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) with a score of 280. That same year, he represented Hubei Province in the Chinese Mathematical Olympiad (CMO), where he earned a third-prize (bronze) award. His earlier achievements include winning a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). He was a member of the Hubei provincial teams for both informatics and mathematics during his high school career.

noi_b_cheng_longju_2009.gold:
Cheng Longju (程龙举) is a Chinese competitive programmer who represented Chongqing Bashu Secondary School (specifically its Luneng Bashu branch). In 2009, he won a bronze medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), scoring 260 points. His performance in national informatics competitions followed earlier successes in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIp), including a third-prize finish in the 2005 Chongqing regional junior division and a first-prize finish in the 2008 senior division.

In 2010, Cheng was admitted to the Science Experimental Class at Renmin University of China through the national recommendation (baosong) program. His academic trajectory was noted in local media for his significant improvement during high school, rising from a lower grade ranking to achieving national-level competition success.

noi_b_miao_peihan_2009.gold:
Miao Peihan (缪沛晗) is a computer scientist and the John E. Savage Assistant Professor of Computer Science at Brown University. She graduated from Jiangsu Changzhou Senior High School, where she competed in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), earning a bronze medal and the title of Best Female Contestant.

She subsequently joined the ACM Honors Class at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, receiving her B.S. in 2014. She earned her Ph.D. in Computer Science from the University of California, Berkeley, in 2019. Her research focuses on cryptography and security, particularly secure multi-party computation. Her work has been recognized with several honors, including the NSF CAREER Award, the Google Research Scholar Award, and the Meta Research Award.

noi_b_xin_chao_2009.gold: Xin Chao (辛超) is a competitive programmer from Guangdong Beijiang Middle School (广东北江中学). In 2009, while a second-year student in senior high school (Grade 11), he represented Guangdong Province in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he won a bronze medal (third prize) with a score of 255. Later that year, as a third-year student (Grade 12), he achieved a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP 2009) in the Guangdong division, recording a score of 290. He previously participated in the 2006 NOIP as a junior high school student from Shaoguan No. 1 Middle School.

noi_s_an_yujie_2009.gold:
An Yujie (安宇杰) is a competitive programmer who earned a silver medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) as a student at Daqing No. 1 Middle School in Heilongjiang Province. Following his success in the olympiad, he was granted recommended admission (baosong) to university in 2010.

He attended the SJTU-UM Joint Institute at Shanghai Jiao Tong University before continuing his studies at the University of Michigan. As a member of the University of Michigan programming team, "The Victors," he competed in the ACM-ICPC World Finals in 2014 and 2015, achieving ranks of 67th and 50th, respectively. He later served as an assistant coach for the university's programming team and co-authored research on parallel and distributed computing.

noi_s_huang_yifeng_2009.gold: Huang Yifeng (黄一峰) is a competitive programmer and mathematician from Chengdu No. 7 High School (四川省成都七中). He won a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) as a tenth-grade student and earned a second silver medal at NOI 2010. His other competitive achievements include a gold medal at the 2010 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO) and multiple first-prize awards in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2011, he was admitted to the School of Mathematical Sciences at Peking University through the national recommendation (*baosong*) program for distinguished competition medalists.

noi_s_li_peiqian_2009.gold:
Li Peiqian (李佩谦) is a competitive programmer who represented Northeast Normal University Affiliated High School (吉林省东北师大附中) in national informatics competitions. He won a silver medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) with a score of 270, ranking 53rd nationally. He previously earned a bronze medal at NOI 2008.

Li consistently achieved top results in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP), securing first-prize honors in the Senior Group in 2006, 2007, and 2009. Beyond informatics, he participated in his school's 2003 grade science experimental class, where he developed an engineering project titled "Smart Wheelchair." He was recognized in the 2008 national list of students recommended for university admission due to his competition achievements.

noi_b_zhang_xue_2009.gold: Zhang Xue (张雪) is a Chinese competitive programmer who represented Mianyang Nanshan High School (四川省绵阳南山中学) in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009). At the competition, held in Beijing, she earned a bronze medal with a score of 250. In addition to her medal, she was honored with the "Best Female Contestant" (最佳女选手奖) award, a distinction she shared with fellow medalist Miao Peihan. At the time of her achievement in 2009, she was a student in the 11th grade (高二).

noi_b_li_bofang_2009.gold:
Li Bofang (李博放) is a competitive programmer who attended Mianyang Nanshan High School in Sichuan Province. He was a two-time bronze medalist at the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), earning third-prize honors in both 2008 and 2009. Additionally, he achieved first-prize rankings in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for three consecutive years from 2007 to 2009.

Based on his competitive record, Li was admitted to Renmin University of China in 2010 as a recommended student (保送生). He enrolled in the university's Science Experimental Class, specializing in Information and Mathematics.

noi_b_ye_dan_2009.gold: Ye Dan (叶丹) is a competitive programmer from Chongqing Bashu Secondary School (重庆市巴蜀中学). As a high school student, she represented Chongqing in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where she earned a bronze medal. In the same year, she received a bronze medal in the China region of the Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO 2009) and won a first prize in the senior category of the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP 2009). She graduated from Bashu Secondary School in 2010.

noi_b_xie_luyu_2009.gold:
Xie Luyu (谢路昱) is a computer scientist and researcher specializing in bioinformatics. He attended Fuzhou No. 1 High School, where he won a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and a bronze medal in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). 

In 2010, he was admitted to Fudan University to study Computer Science and Technology. During his undergraduate studies, he represented Fudan University in the 2011 ACM-ICPC World Finals, where his team placed 38th. After graduating in 2014, he pursued doctoral studies at the National University of Singapore, earning a PhD in Computer Science in 2020 with a focus on genome assembly and next-generation sequencing (NGS) data analysis. He subsequently worked as a data scientist at the National Cancer Centre Singapore and later became an associate researcher at the Institute of Dataspace, Hefei Comprehensive National Science Center.

noi_b_guo_xiaoxu_2009.gold:
Guo Xiaoxu (郭晓旭) is a competitive programmer who earned a bronze medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) as a student at Guangdong Chaoyang Experimental School. He graduated from high school in 2011 and subsequently attended Shanghai Jiao Tong University (SJTU).

Guo achieved international prominence in the ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest (ICPC). Representing SJTU's "Mithril" team, he won a gold medal, placed second overall (runner-up), and was named the Asian Champion at the 37th ACM-ICPC World Finals in 2013. Known by the online handle "ftiasch," he is a highly ranked participant on competitive programming platforms including Codeforces, AtCoder, and Topcoder. His earlier honors include a first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP).

noi_b_wang_enze_2009.gold:
Wang Enze (王恩泽) is a computer science professional and former competitive programmer from Inner Mongolia. As a student at Baotou No. 9 Middle School (内蒙古包头市第九中学), he represented his region in the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), where he earned a bronze medal with a score of 250. 

He subsequently pursued higher education at Taiyuan University of Technology, enrolling in the Department of Computer Science and Technology. He graduated with a bachelor's degree in 2015. Following his graduation, he joined the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China. He has been recognized by his alma mater as a notable alumnus for his career transition from technical competition to public service.

noi_b_liang_jingyuan_2009.gold:
Liang Jingyuan (梁景元) is a competitive programmer from Guangxi, China. He attended the Foreign Language School Attached to Guangxi Normal University (广西师大附外). 

In 2008, while in his second year of high school (Grade 11), Liang won a first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) within the Guangxi region. The following year, he represented his province at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he earned a bronze medal. He was recognized as a prominent student in informatics at his school during this period.

noi_b_lyu_rundong_2009.gold: Lyu Rundong (吕润东) is a competitive programmer from Chongqing, China. As a student at Chongqing No. 8 Secondary School (重庆市重庆八中), he competed in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he earned a bronze medal. Following his success in informatics competitions, he was admitted to either Peking University or Tsinghua University in 2010, as recorded in his secondary school's official list of graduates accepted into China's top two universities.

noi_b_li_bojie_2009.gold:
Li Bojie (李博杰) is a computer scientist and entrepreneur who earned a bronze medal (69th nationwide) at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while attending Shijiazhuang No. 2 Middle School. He is the author of the widely circulated informatics competition guide *Pianfen Daolun* (骗分导论).

Li graduated from the School of the Gifted Young at the University of Science and Technology of China (USTC) and obtained his Ph.D. in 2019 through a joint program between USTC and Microsoft Research Asia. In 2019, he joined Huawei’s "Genius Youth" program, serving as a deputy chief expert in the 2012 Laboratories. He later transitioned to AI entrepreneurship, becoming the Chief Scientist at Pine AI. His research in networked systems has been published in top-tier conferences including SIGCOMM and SOSP.

noi_b_fan_rundong_2009.gold:
Fan Rundong (樊润冬) is a Chinese game developer and co-founder of Hypergryph (上海鹰角网络科技有限公司). He attended Urumqi No. 1 Senior High School, where he earned a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In 2010, he was recommended for admission to the School of Software at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, where he completed his bachelor's degree in 2014 and his master's degree in 2016.

In 2017, Fan co-founded Hypergryph alongside fellow alumni and served as the lead game designer for the company's flagship mobile title, *Arknights* (明日方舟). In 2023, he was honored as an "Outstanding Alumnus" by the Shanghai Jiao Tong University School of Software for his achievements in the gaming industry.

noi_b_xu_ranci_2009.gold:
Xu Ranci (徐苒茨) is a computer science educator and former competitive programmer. While a student at Sichuan Mianyang Nanshan High School, he won a bronze medal (third prize) at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and twice qualified for the Sichuan provincial team. Based on his competition record, he was recommended for admission to Fudan University, where he earned both his bachelor’s and master’s degrees in computer science.

He currently serves as an informatics competition coach at Chengdu Jiaxiang Foreign Language School (also known as Jiaxiang Innovation School). In his professional career, he has received recognition for his teaching, including winning second prize in the 8th CCF NOI Computer Programming Segment Teaching Competition.

noi_b_zhu_bingqi_2009.gold:
Zhu Bingqi (朱炳琪) is a competitive programmer and legal scholar who attended Hengshui High School in Hebei Province. In 2009, she won a bronze medal at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) senior group. 

In 2010, she was recommended for admission to Shandong University, where she enrolled in the Information Security (Secrecy Management) program. During her undergraduate studies, she was active in the university's Law School, representing Shandong University in prestigious international competitions, including the Philip C. Jessup International Law Moot Court Competition and the Price Media Law Moot Court. She later pursued graduate studies in law, earning a master's degree from East China University of Political Science and Law in 2020.

noi_b_chen_yi_2009.gold: Chen Yi (陈怡) is a competitive programmer from Chongqing, China. As a student at Chongqing No. 8 High School (重庆市重庆八中), she represented the Chongqing provincial team in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), where she earned a bronze medal. In the same year, she also achieved a first-prize award in the Senior Group of the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP 2009). She was recognized as one of the school's notable competition achievers during her secondary education.

noi_b_zou_xun_2009.gold:
Zou Xun (邹逊) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer. He attended Bengbu No. 2 Middle School (蚌埠二中) in Anhui Province, where he distinguished himself in informatics competitions. In 2008, he ranked second in Anhui Province in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) with a score of 330. He was subsequently selected for the Anhui provincial team and won a bronze medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In 2010, he received the Best Nomination Award for the Bengbu Mayor's Innovation Award.

In 2011, Zou was admitted to Fudan University as a recommended student (baosong) to major in Computer Science and Technology. Representing Fudan University in the 2013 ACM International Collegiate Programming Contest (ACM-ICPC) Asia Regional Contest in Chengdu, his team earned a gold medal, finishing in ninth place.

noi_b_li_yi_2009.gold:
Li Yi (李艺) is a Chinese competitive programmer who achieved distinction in national informatics competitions. While a student at the High School Affiliated to Shandong Normal University (山东师大附中), he earned a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and a bronze medal (third prize) at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) with a score of 220. 

He subsequently represented Yantai Development Zone High School (烟台开发区高级中学), where he won a silver medal (second prize) at NOI 2010 and participated in the 2010 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO). Due to his competition record, he was granted eligibility for recommended university admission (保送) in 2011.

noi_b_rao_guoteng_2009.gold:
Rao Guoteng (饶国腾) is a competitive programmer and software engineer. While attending Chengdu No. 7 High School, he won a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and a bronze medal in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He also represented Sichuan in the NOI 2009 team competition, earning a third prize.

Rao pursued his undergraduate studies at Shanghai Jiao Tong University as a member of the ACM Class. During his university years, he competed in the ACM-ICPC, winning a silver medal at the 2011 Shanghai Regional. Professionally, he has served as a senior software engineer at Google and later joined the autonomous driving company Pony.ai as a software engineer.

noi_b_zhang_chong_2009.gold:
Zhang Chong (张冲) is a Chinese competitive programmer who attended Shaanxi Normal University High School (陕西师范大学附属中学). In 2009, while in his second year of high school, he competed as a formal representative of Shaanxi Province in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he earned a bronze medal with a score of 207. 

Based on his achievements in informatics competitions, including a first-prize finish in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP), he was admitted to Zhejiang University (浙江大学) in 2010 as a recommended student (保送生).

noi_b_li_runming_2009.gold: Li Runming (李润铭) is a competitive programmer who attended Beijing No. 80 High School (北京市第八十中学). He earned a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP), he achieved first-prize honors in the Beijing region in both 2009 and 2010. Following his high school career, he was admitted to the School of Mathematical Sciences at Peking University in 2011. He is distinct from the Chinese national volleyball player of the same name.

noi_b_cai_ning_2009.gold: Cai Ning (蔡凝) is a competitive programmer from Jiangxi Province, China. As a student at Jiujiang No. 1 Middle School (江西省九江一中), he represented his province in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009). Competing as a high school junior (Grade 11), he achieved a total score of 202 and was awarded a bronze medal. He also earned a first-prize award in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) senior group.

noi_b_he_boshuo_2009.gold:
He Boshuo (何博硕) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer who attended the High School Affiliated to Renmin University of China (RDFZ). He earned a bronze medal (third prize) at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and previously won a bronze medal at the 2008 NOI. During high school, he also received first-prize honors in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in 2007, 2008, and 2009, and won a second prize in the 2009 National High School Mathematics League.

He pursued his undergraduate studies at Tsinghua University in the Department of Computer Science and Technology. In 2013, he was a member of the team that received the "Outstanding Winner" designation, the highest award, in the Mathematical Contest in Modeling (MCM/ICM).

noi_b_zhong_yin_2009.gold:
Zhong Yin (钟音) is a computer scientist who attended Daqing Experimental High School (黑龙江省大庆实验中学) in Heilongjiang Province. He was a competitive programmer, winning a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) and a bronze medal (third prize) in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). 

In 2010, he was admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Tsinghua University. During his undergraduate studies, he contributed as a reviewer and provided technical feedback for the book *Node.js Development Guide* (*Node.js开发指南*), published in 2012. He was part of the Tsinghua computer science cohort that included notable developers such as Guo Jiabao (BYVoid).

noi_b_yang_shubai_2009.gold:
Yang Shubai (杨舒柏) is a researcher specializing in aero-engine control and performance modeling. While a student at Urumqi No. 1 Senior High School in Xinjiang, he earned a bronze medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He pursued higher education at Beihang University, where he received a bachelor's degree in aircraft propulsion engineering in 2014 and a PhD in aerospace propulsion theory and engineering in 2020. 

Following a postdoctoral fellowship at Beihang University, Yang joined the Institute of Aero-engine at Tsinghua University as an assistant researcher in 2023. His research focuses on nonlinear control methods, multi-disciplinary coupling systems, and performance simulation for aircraft engines. He has authored numerous academic papers on limit protection and sliding mode control for aero-engines.

noi_b_alafate_2009.gold:
Alafate (阿拉法特), also known as Alafate Jureti (阿拉法特·居来提), is a computer scientist and a 2009 bronze medalist in the China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He attended Urumqi No. 1 Senior High School (新疆乌鲁木齐一中), where he was a prominent competitive programmer, also earning a bronze medal in NOI 2007 and a first prize in the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP).

In 2010, he was recommended for admission to Fudan University, where he majored in Computer Science and Technology. He graduated in 2014 as an outstanding graduate representative. He subsequently pursued doctoral studies at the University of California, San Diego (UCSD), earning a PhD in Computer Science in 2020 under the supervision of Yoav Freund. His research focused on parallel boosting and machine learning. Following his PhD, he joined Facebook (now Meta) as a research scientist in Menlo Park, California.

noi_b_yi_qian_2009.gold:
Yi Qian (易茜) is a Chinese competitive programmer who represented Changsha No. 1 High School (湖南省长沙市一中) in informatics competitions. In 2009, she earned a bronze medal (third prize) at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), achieving a score of 200 points. 

During her high school career, she was a consistent top performer in regional competitions, securing first-prize honors in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for Hunan in both 2008 and 2009. At the time of her 2009 national achievement, she was a Grade 11 student. Her performance contributed to the competitive record of Changsha No. 1 High School, a leading institution in China's informatics olympiad circuit.

noi_b_liang_jinhong_2009.gold:
Liang Jinhong (梁錦鴻) is a competitive programmer from Macau who attended Hou Kong Middle School (澳門濠江中學). He represented Macau at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), where he earned a bronze medal with a score of 190. 

Prior to his national achievement, Liang was a consistent participant in the Macau Olympiad in Informatics (MOI). In 2008, he won a gold medal at the MOI, ranking 7th among 42 participants. He also secured a bronze medal in the 2007 MOI, where he placed 23rd. His performance in these regional competitions led to his selection for the Macau representative team at the national level.

noi_b_zhu_xudong_2009.gold:
Zhu Xudong (朱旭东) is a competitive programmer who represented Chongqing Bashu Secondary School (重庆市巴蜀中学) in national informatics competitions. In 2009, while in the eleventh grade, he competed as an official contestant in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) held in Beijing. He earned a total of 197 points, securing a bronze medal. 

Prior to his national success, Zhu was active in regional competitions, notably winning a first prize in the Senior Group (提高组) of the 2008 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) representing the same school. His performance in these competitions established him as a prominent student programmer from the Chongqing region during his high school years.

noi_b_ning_ke_2009.gold: Ning Ke (宁可) is a competitive programmer from Jiangxi Province, China. He attended Jiujiang No. 1 Middle School (江西省九江一中), where he was a member of the school's informatics competition team. In 2009, he represented his school and province at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he won a bronze medal. During his time at Jiujiang No. 1 Middle School, he was coached by Gong Yu (龚禹), who was recognized by the China Computer Federation as an outstanding instructor in 2009 for his contributions to the school's informatics program.

noi_b_hou_jiaxu_2009.gold:
Hou Jiaxu (侯佳旭) is a Chinese computer science competitor who attended Zhengzhou 101 Middle School (郑州市第一0一中学) in Henan Province. In 2009, while in his second year of high school, he represented Henan at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009). He earned a bronze medal in the competition with a total score of 190 points. 

Following his achievement in the national olympiad, Hou was included in the 2010 list of students from Henan Province recommended for admission to higher education institutions without the standard entrance examination (baosong). He was subsequently admitted to Northwestern Polytechnical University (西北工业大学).

noi_b_wei_zhenggong_2009.gold: Wei Zhenggong (魏正功) is a competitive programmer from Hebei Province, China. As a student at Tangshan No. 1 High School (河北省唐山一中), he was active in informatics competitions. In 2008, he earned a first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for the Hebei region. He subsequently represented his province as a formal contestant in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he won a bronze medal (Third Prize) with a total score of 184.

noi_b_dong_yuqian_2009.gold:
Dong Yuqian (董宇茜) is a computer scientist and technology executive. She represented Harbin Normal University Attached High School (黑龙江省哈师大附中) at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), where she earned a bronze medal.

She pursued her higher education at Tsinghua University within the Institute for Interdisciplinary Information Sciences (IIIS). During her time at Tsinghua, she was recommended for admission to the Master’s program in Computer Science and Technology and was active in the institute's research and athletic activities. Her academic work included research on power consumption control for large-scale data centers. Professionally, she serves as a director at Ahi-Fintech (慧安金科), a Beijing-based financial technology company that specializes in artificial intelligence and risk management solutions.

noi_b_peng_yuhan_2009.gold:
Peng Yuhan (彭禹涵) is a computer scientist and competitive programmer who attended Anhui Wuhu No. 1 Middle School. In 2009, as a second-year high school student, he won a bronze medal at the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and was a member of the Anhui provincial team. He also secured first-prize honors in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in both 2007 and 2008.

He later pursued a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science at Hong Kong Baptist University (HKBU). During his undergraduate studies, he represented HKBU in several international competitions, winning the First Runner-up award at the 2012 ACM Collegiate Programming Contest (Hong Kong) and a preliminary award at the 2013 Asia Student Supercomputer Challenge (ASC13). He graduated with First Class Honours in 2014.

noi_b_fan_hongbo_2009.gold: Fan Hongbo (范鸿博) is a Chinese computer science competitor who attended the First附属中学 of Central China Normal University (湖北省华师一附中). In 2008, he earned a first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). Representing Hubei Province as a high school junior, he competed in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), where he was awarded a bronze medal (third prize).

noi_b_xing_jianan_2009.gold:
Xing Jianan (邢佳楠) is a Chinese competitive programmer who attended Beijing No. 80 High School (北京第八十中学). He was a student of the prominent informatics coach Jia Zhiyong. Xing achieved significant success in the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) series, winning first prizes in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for three consecutive years from 2007 to 2009. 

In 2009, while in his second year of high school, he represented Beijing at the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he earned a bronze medal. Due to his consistent performance in national competitions, he was granted "recommended" (保送) status for university admission in 2010 and was subsequently admitted to Peking University.

noi_b_sha_mo_2009.gold:
Sha Mo (沙漠) is a Chinese informatics competitor who graduated from Dalian Yuming Senior High School (辽宁省大连育明高中) in 2010. He previously attended Dalian Boya Middle School. 

In 2009, while a student at Dalian Yuming, he won a bronze medal at the 26th China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) as a member of the Liaoning provincial team. That same year, he earned a national first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). Following his success in these competitions, he was admitted to the Harbin Institute of Technology (哈尔滨工业大学) in 2010 through the university's recommendation (baosong) program for distinguished student competitors.

noi_b_xu_lunbo_2009.gold:
Xu Lunbo (许伦博) is a computer scientist and former competitive programmer from Hainan, China. While attending Hainan Middle School, he won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and a first prize in the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP). In 2008, he received the Excellence Award at the inaugural S.-T. Yau High School Mathematics Awards for his research on diagonal intersection points in regular polygons.

In 2010, Xu was admitted to the Department of Computer Science and Technology at Peking University as a recommended student. During his undergraduate studies, he co-authored research on smartphone-assisted drowsy driving detection and won a second prize in the 2012 Microsoft Student Challenge for a Kinect-controlled robotic fish project. He also received a Best Poster Award at the 2013 Peking University Information Science Undergraduate Research Exhibition.

noi_b_wang_sida_2009.gold:
Wang Sida (王思达) is a software engineer and entrepreneur who was a bronze medalist at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) representing Xi'an Tieyi High School (陕西省西安铁一中). Due to his achievements in informatics competitions, including a national first prize in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP), he was recommended for admission (保送) to Xi'an Jiaotong University in 2010.

After earning his bachelor's degree from Xi'an Jiaotong University, he obtained a master's degree from Rice University in the United States. His professional career includes roles as a software engineer at Tencent, LinkedIn, and Airbnb. He later founded BuzzBreak, a news aggregation platform focused on the Southeast Asian market, where he serves as CEO.

noi_b_feng_dan_2009.gold:
Feng Dan (冯丹) is a Chinese competitive programmer who attended the No. 2 High School of East China Normal University (HSEFZ) in Shanghai. She achieved national recognition in informatics, winning a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while in her first year of high school. She followed this with another bronze medal at the 2010 NOI and participated in the 2010 China Team Selection Contest (CTSC). 

In 2011, she was admitted to Tsinghua University as a recommended student (baosong) following her achievements in the informatics olympiad. During her high school career, she also contributed to the competitive programming community by providing testing programs for competition problems, such as "Interstellar Exploration" (星际探险).

noi_b_shen_haoquan_2009.gold:
Shen Haoquan (申皓全) is a computer scientist and engineer who first gained recognition as a competitive programmer. While attending Shanxi University Affiliated High School (山西大学附中), he won a bronze medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and multiple first prizes in the provincial-level NOIP (2007, 2008).

He pursued his undergraduate studies at Zhejiang University, where he was a member of the Chu Kochen Honors College and the "Qiushi Science Class" for computer science. During his university years, he continued competing in programming contests and began research in computer vision, co-authoring work on unsupervised video adaptation for human motion parsing. Professionally, he served as an engineer at the computer vision startup DeepGlint (格灵深瞳) and has been credited as an inventor on patents related to action detection and person recognition.

noi_b_liu_yingshan_2009.gold: Liu Yingshan (刘颖珊) is a competitive programmer from Guangdong, China. As a student at Zhongshan Memorial Middle School (广东省中山纪念中学), she won a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In December of the same year, while in her second year of high school, she earned a first prize in the Senior Group of the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP).

noi_b_jin_lingzi_2009.gold: Jin Lingzi (金凌子) is a Chinese computer science student and competitive programmer. As a student at Zhejiang Wenzhou High School (浙江省温州中学), she competed in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI), where she earned a bronze medal. In 2010, she was admitted to Fudan University as a recommended student (保送生) to study Computer Science and Technology, a placement granted in recognition of her achievement in the national informatics competition.

noi_b_yan_jiabao_2009.gold:
Yan Jiabao (闫家宝) is a Chinese competitive programmer who represented Henan University High School (河大附中) in national informatics competitions. He earned a bronze medal at the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and competed again in the 2010 NOI as a senior. 

Following his success in the informatics olympiads, Yan was admitted to Tsinghua University in 2011 through the recommended student (baosong) program, a common pathway for high-achieving olympiad medalists in China. He is distinguished from namesakes in academia, such as Professor Yan Jiabao (严加宝) of Tianjin University, by the specific Chinese characters of his surname and his documented competition record.

noi_b_li_song_2009.gold: Li Song (李松) is a competitive programmer from Hainan Province, China. As a student at Hainan Lingao Middle School (海南省临高中学), he competed in the 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) while in the ninth grade (初三), where he earned a bronze medal. Following this achievement, he continued to represent his school in informatics competitions, winning a first prize in the 2010 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) Senior Group for the Hainan region.

noi_b_zhao_zhiheng_2009.gold:
Zhao Zhiheng (赵志恒) is a competitive programmer from Jilin Province, China. He attended the High School Attached to Northeast Normal University (吉林省东北师大附中). 

Zhao achieved significant recognition in the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) and related competitions. As a ninth-grade student, he won a bronze medal at the 2009 NOI. He continued his competitive success in high school, earning another bronze medal at the 2010 NOI and a bronze medal at the 2011 Asia-Pacific Informatics Olympiad (APIO). In 2011, while in the eleventh grade, he competed in the 28th NOI and achieved a score of 460, which earned him a silver medal. He was a member of the Jilin provincial team for multiple years during his secondary education.

noi_b_sun_su_2009.gold: Sun Su (孙溯) is a competitive programmer from Liaoning Province, China. As a student at Dalian Yuming Senior High School (辽宁省大连育明高中), he represented the Liaoning provincial team in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009), where he earned a bronze medal. He was also a member of the 2009 national informatics winter camp. Following his success in informatics competitions, he was admitted to the Georgia Institute of Technology in the United States, appearing on his high school's 2010–2011 college admission honor roll.

noi_s_li_guanru_2009.gold: Li Guanru (李冠儒) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer. As a student at Tianjin Nankai Middle School (天津市天津南开中学), he achieved significant recognition in informatics competitions. In 2009, he won a silver medal at the 26th China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) with a score of 280. He continued his competitive success in 2010, earning a first prize in the Tianjin region of the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP 2010) while in his second year of high school. His performance in these national-level competitions established him as a top student in his cohort at Nankai Middle School, a prestigious institution known for its strong record in academic olympiads.

noi_s_liu_ke_2009.gold: Liu Ke (刘坷) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer. As a student at Hunan Normal University High School (湖南师大附中), he won a silver medal at the 2009 China National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). He also achieved a first prize in the 2008 and 2009 National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) in Hunan. Due to his achievements in informatics competitions, he was eligible for recommendation (保送) to top-tier universities in China. He was part of a high-achieving cohort at Hunan Normal University High School that included several international and national olympiad medalists.

noi_b_yan_lifeng_2009.gold:
Yan Lifeng (颜立峰) is a Chinese computer scientist and competitive programmer. As a student at Liuzhou Senior High School (广西省柳州高中) in Guangxi, he participated in the 26th National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI 2009) held in Beijing. Competing as a high school sophomore (Grade 11), he earned a bronze medal (third prize) with a total score of 167.

Beyond his high school competition record, Yan is distinct from namesakes in academia, such as the professor of chemical physics at the University of Science and Technology of China. His verified achievements are centered on his performance in the 2009 national informatics competition representing his school and province.

noi_b_tan_chuanqi_2009.gold:
Tan Chuanqi (谭传奇) is a Chinese computer scientist and informatics competitor. As a student at Wuhan No. 2 High School (湖北省武汉二中), he achieved notable success in the National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI). In July 2009, while in the 11th grade, he won a bronze medal at the 26th NOI. Later that year, in December 2009, he earned a first prize in the senior group of the National Olympiad in Informatics in Provinces (NOIP) for Hubei Province.

Following his high school career, he pursued advanced research in artificial intelligence. He completed a joint doctoral program between Beihang University and Microsoft Research Asia (MSRA), graduating in 2019. He subsequently joined the Alibaba DAMO Academy as an algorithm expert, where his research focuses on natural language processing, deep transfer learning, and large-scale language models. He has co-authored influential works, including a widely cited survey on deep transfer learning.
